entry.title: SAP UX Q1/2026 Update – Part 8: SAP Customer Experience: Mobile Daily Catch-Up for Sales
entry.content:
The new Daily Catch-Up within the SAP Sales Cloud mobile app is a real game changer for sales reps, since it directly helps you sell more, by helping you prioritize your day, with a three-step flow highlighting upcoming activities, missed items from the day before, and high-priority deals and leads that otherwise would fall through the cracks. Not only that: it is delightful to use, with nice animations and haptics.

This series of blog posts covers innovations in user experience in our public cloud products from the last six months or so. In case you missed it, Part 1 provides general updates about Joule, SAP Work Zone and SAP Mobile Start, along with links to all the other parts.

 

Revolutionizing the Sales Experience with Daily Catch-Up

Daily Catch-Up is a major user experience innovation designed specifically for mobile sales reps, and is another example of how we are making work form enjoyable, helping users achieve better outcomes with AI.

We’ve learnt from extensive user research and conversations with customers that sales reps often struggle to prioritize their day due to "scattered signals" that surface throughout the day across emails, chats, and various notification channels. The Daily Catch-Up addresses this "notification overload" by unifying these signals into one predictable, guided experience.

Figure 1 gives a first impression: the first two examples show how you can access the catch-up: via a notification, or from the daily summary card on the SAP Sales Cloud apps’s start page. The third example shows the catch-up screen.

Figure 1: Notification and Daily Summary on home screen lead you to your daily catch-up. Alt Text: Three images of mobile phones, as described in the text. The third example shows a card “Visit Kixo Plant” with date and time, and a “Why Prioritized?” section, statint it is starting in 2 hours, with a “Reschedule” and “AI Summary” button and “Mark as Complete”.

Enjoyable: Intuitive and Tactile Design

The new Daily Catch-Up reimagines the interaction model for mobile CRM by moving away from traditional, cluttered lists to a modern, card-based layout.

  • Reduced Cognitive Load: By showing only one card at a time, we keep your attention focused on what matters most without the distraction of competing items. Each card has a clear front and back. A simple tap reveals additional details only when the user needs them, keeping the primary view clean and focussed.
  • Tactile Design: Swiping through cards feels natural, quick, and thumb-friendly, with almost no learning curve. You can use directional swipes to instantly mark tasks as complete or save them for later, without extra taps or navigation.
  • Delightful Experience: Smooth animations and subtle haptics add a touch of delight and give the flow a light, game-like quality. The overall experience feels faster, more intuitive, and more enjoyable on a small mobile screen – as you can see in the animation in Figure 2 below.

Figure 2: Examples of the animations for flipping the card, and for swiping to the next one within a step. Alt Text: Video of a mobile phone, showing how the card rotates around a vertical axis when tapped on, and how you can swipe it upwards towards top right.

Better Outcomes: Heuristic Prioritization and “Top 5 Focus”

We are helping Sales Reps move beyond simple chronological tracking to achieve better business outcomes through smart prioritization.

  • Heuristic Scoring: This new version of Catch-Up uses a scoring system that combines machine learning inputs and local context to calculate a priority score. This ensures Reps see what is most urgent and impactful first.
  • The "Top 5 Focus": Each morning, the system surfaces a "Top 5 Focus" list of prioritized items across opportunities, leads, quotes, orders, accounts or cases that require immediate attention. Ranking follows the same principles of business impact and urgency. Each card includes a short, two-line guidance summary to provide instant clarity.

One Consolidated, Guided Flow

Catch-Up brings everything together, providing a single, predictable way for users to start their day. There are two ways for you to get started: from contextual notifications, or from the app’s homepage, as shown in Figure 3 below:

Figure 3: Contextual notifications and the Daily Summary card on the home page lead you to Daily Catch-Up. Alt Text: Two images of mobile phone. On the left, schematically three notifications are shown on the start screen: “Start Your Day, Focussed”; “You’re at San Francisco International Airport – Do you want to review today’s schedule or follow-ups while you wait?”; “You’re near multiple accounts/leads but don’t have a visit scheduled – Do you want to create a visit?”. On the right the start page, with a Daily Summary card for catch-up, stating “Nice work! You’ve kepe up your Catch-up streak for 3 days straight!”.

Catch-Up has a Three-Step Guided Flow:

  1. Upcoming activities: Highlighting what’s next on the calendar.
  2. Missed items: Covering anything that was overlooked the previous day.
  3. “Top 5 focus”: the high priority items, ranked as described above, that often fall through the cracks because they do not appear in the upcoming activity list.

Figure 4 below shows an animation of how users can move from step 2 to step 3 in the guided flow. You see that on the last card in step 2 you have action buttons to set the status or log a call. Once you swipe it away, you see an example of our new illustration design, with a message saying that CRM is now up-to-date. Clicking on Next Step brings you to step 3, with a prioritized deal. Each card has a “Why Prioritized?” section, explaining why the system has chosen to prioritize this item.

Figure 4: Moving from Step 2 to Step 3. Alt Text: Video of a mobile phone, showing the user swiping up the last card in step 2, getting a message that “Your CRM is now up-to-date – move to next step”, clicking on “Next step” brings up a message “Terry, here are the top 5 things for you to focus on today”, showing a card with the first one.

With Daily Catch-Up, the SAP Sales Cloud mobile app becomes a tool that really helps you sell more, rather than feeling like a tracking tool that your company wants you to use. It becomes a "habit builder" that helps you hit your quotas and stay focused on your real day-to-day goals.

Find out more in the documentation:

  • Mobile: Daily Catch-Up.

Get the SAP Sales Cloud mobile app in one of the app stores:

  • SAP Mobile Sales in Apple app store.
  • SAP Mobile Sales in Google Play.

 

Continued soon…

I hope you enjoyed this eigth part, covering SAP Customer Experience – SAP Sales Cloud.

I will post part nine next week, covering latest UX innovations for UI technologies.

Part 1 of the series lists all the parts, and links to those parts that have already been published:

  • SAP UX Q1/2026 Update – Part 1: AI, Joule, SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Mobile Start.

Do keep posting your experiences and recommendations yourself in the SAP Community, with the SAP Fiori and/or the User Experience tag! In case you are wondering how to get a list of the most recent blog posts on SAP Fiori and User Experience in our new SAP Community, use these links:

  • Latest blog posts on SAP Fiori in the SAP Community.
  • Latest blog posts on User Experience in the SAP Community.

entry.title: Impact of Material Type in MM01 on SAP Configuration and Processes
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to better understand the technical and functional impact of the Material Type defined in MM01.

From my understanding, material type controls several aspects of the material master and logistics processes, such as:

  • Number range assignment

  • Allowed views in the material master

  • Procurement type

  • Quantity/value updating

  • Account determination in FI

  • Field selection in material master

  • Integration with other modules

However, I would like to understand in more detail:

  1. Which SAP tables and configuration objects are directly influenced by the material type?

  2. How does the material type affect valuation, accounting, and stock management behavior?

  3. What is the relationship between material type and account determination (OBYC)?

  4. How does it influence procurement processes (purchasing vs production)?

  5. Are there any best practices for defining custom material types (Z material types) in SAP MM?

I would appreciate a deeper explanation of the technical and configuration impact of material type within SAP MM and its integration with FI and other logistics modules.

entry.title: Re: consumption group in mm02
entry.content: MM02 is a master data transaction and does not display or control movement types. If you want to analyze consumption by the new group, the right approach is to enhance reporting logic (e.g., CDS views or KPI dashboards) so that all relevant movement types are classified under the new group. You can optionally add a custom field in the material master to tag the material with the group, but the consumption itself will always be visible only in transactional reports, not in MM02

entry.title: SAP GROW – Service Entry Sheet does not recognize quantity
entry.content:
Hello

We kindly request SAP to:

  1. Confirm whether the described purchase order model is supported for Service Entry Sheet processing in SAP GROW
  2. Clarify the recommended service model for fixed-price services that require service entry and approval
  3. Inform whether there is any:
    • SAP Note
    • KBA
    • Known product limitation
  4. Provide SAP best practice guidance to avoid this issue in future purchase orders

 Business Impact

This issue prevents the registration and approval of already executed services, directly impacting:

  • Procurement operations
  • Service approval process
  • Supplier invoicing and payments

 

 

 

Thanks


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP GROW – Service Entry Sheet does not recognize qua...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Thank you for your detailed analysis.

  1. Supportability of the Purchase Order Model
    The described purchase order model is supported in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and GROW with SAP when service procurement is created using service lines in the PO (Item Category K – Service). The Service Entry Sheet (SES) determines the value based on the quantity entered in SES and the pricing maintained in the PO service lines.

  2. Recommended Service Model for Fixed-Price Services
    SAP recommends using the Lean Service Procurement model, where:

  • Service lines are maintained in the Purchase Order

  • SES is created with actual quantities entered at the service line level

  • The system automatically calculates the value based on PO pricing.

  1. Relevant SAP Documentation
    You may refer to the following SAP Knowledge Base Articles:

  • KBA 3685262 – Service Entry Sheet cannot be entered by amount

  • KBA 3434955 – Net Order Price shows 0.00 in Service Entry Sheet

These articles explain that SES valuation is quantity-based and the amount is calculated automatically from the PO price.

  1. SAP Best Practice
    To avoid similar issues in future purchase orders, SAP recommends:

  • Maintaining service lines with quantity and price in the PO

  • Entering quantities at the service line level during SES creation

  • Ensuring price unit and quantity are aligned for correct calculation

Following this standard service procurement approach will ensure that SES values are correctly calculated and posted.

Kind regards.

SAP SUPPORT

entry.title: Re: #CoE#TFAL Treatment of Opening Balances After Histori...
entry.content:
Good day @PDIEGO,

Your approach is valid and aligns with the standard mid-year go-live pattern for S/4HANA Public Cloud. Let me add a few clarifications that may help you and others reading this.

Your design is the intended approach, not a workaround.

If your cutover falls mid-FY2026 (period 6), then Historical Balance for pre-cutover periods + G/L Balance & Open Items at the cutover point is exactly how SAP designed the migration to work. This is the standard pattern for any go-live that doesn't fall on fiscal year start.

Clarification 1: Reversal happens at the migration key date, not at fiscal year-end.

Historical balances reverse at the date configured in the Define Settings for Legacy Data Transfer app (SSCUI FINS_MIG_CTRL_1), not automatically at year-end. In your case, this date should be the end of period 5/FY2026 (= end of September 2025 in your April–March calendar). Both FY2025 and FY2026 period 1–5 historical balances reverse at this single point. Misconfiguring this date breaks the entire design.

→ SAP KBA 2754615 – Migration Cockpit: Posting Date / Migration Key Date

Clarification 2: The G/L Balance load at cutover must carry the full cumulative balance.

Since all historical balances net to zero at the migration key date, the G/L Balance & Open Items load in period 6 must represent your complete balance sheet position as of cutover — not a delta or movement since the historical load. This is the single most common mistake in this scenario.

→ Legacy Data Transfer Options – SAP Help (latest/2602)

Clarification 3: Don't forget sub-ledger open items.

G/L Balance & Open Items alone won't give you a complete productive opening. You also need separate migration objects for:

  • FI – Accounts Receivable Open Item for customer receivables
  • FI – Accounts Payable Open Item for vendor payables
  • Fixed Asset (incl. balances and transactions) for asset sub-ledger

Without these, your sub-ledger detail won't match GL totals and downstream processes (dunning, payment runs, depreciation) won't work correctly.

→ Migration Objects for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (latest/2602)

Clarification 4: BCF vs. NPM — choose deliberately.

For the Historical Balance control parameter, Balance Carry Forward (BCF) gives cleaner cumulative period-level reporting in Display G/L Account Balances. Next Period Movement (NPM) works better for P&L period movements. For a full fiscal year like your FY2025, consider using both across separate migration projects if reporting clarity matters to your business.

→ SAP KBA 2943035 – Migration of Historical Balances → Historical Balances – SAP Help (latest/2602)

In summary: your approach is sound and standard for mid-year go-lives. Key things to double-check: (1) migration key date is set to the exact cutover point, (2) the GL Balance load carries the full cumulative position, and (3) sub-ledger open items are migrated with their dedicated migration objects.

For a good end-to-end overview of the migration process, see also this SAP Community blog: Historical Balances Migration in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition.

Best regards,

Marcela (SAP Core Finance team)

entry.title: Configuring Concurrent Login Control with SAP Cloud Identity Services
entry.content:
What Is Concurrent Login

Concurrent login refers to a situation where a single user account maintains multiple active sessions simultaneously, typically from different browsers, devices, or locations.

For example, a user might be logged in at the same time from:

  • Laptop browser

  • Mobile phone

  • Another workstation

Without restrictions, the system allows all sessions to remain active.

Example scenario:

  • User Account
    1. Browser Session (Laptop)
    2. Browser Session (Desktop)
    3. Mobile Session

Allowing unlimited concurrent sessions may introduce several risks:

  • Account sharing between multiple individuals

  • Security exposure if credentials are compromised

  • Difficulty enforcing session governance

  • Potential licensing misuse

To mitigate these risks, organizations often implement concurrent session restrictions through their identity provider.

 

Concurrent Login Control in SAP Cloud Identity Services

SAP Cloud Identity Services includes Identity Authentication Service (IAS), which manages authentication and session control for SAP cloud applications.

IAS allows administrators to configure concurrent access policies that control how many active sessions a user may have at the same time.

When a concurrent login attempt occurs, CIS can enforce different behaviors depending on the configuration.

Typical behaviors include:

ModeBehavior
AllowMultiple sessions are allowed
WarningUser is notified about an existing session and may terminate it
ErrorOnly one active session is permitted

This control is enforced during the authentication process before access is granted to the target application.

 

Configuring Concurrent Login in SAP Cloud Identity Services

Concurrent login behavior can be configured directly in the SAP Cloud Identity Services Administration Console.

Step 1 – Access the CIS Administration Console

Log in to the SAP Cloud Identity Services Admin Console with administrator privileges.

https://tenantID.accounts.ondemand.com/admin

 

Step 2 – Navigate to the Application

Go to Applications:

 

 
Select the application for which you want to control concurrent sessions. Example: SAP Ariba

 

Step 3 – Configure Concurrent Access

Within the application configuration, locate the Authentication and Access section.

 

The default selection is Allow as below:

 

Select the appropriate policy based on your organization's security requirements.

Check the configuration again:

 

Validation Example Using SAP Ariba

To verify the configuration, we can test the behavior using SAP Ariba, which is integrated with SAP Cloud Identity Services and Corporate Identity Provider Entra ID for authentication.

The authentication flow is as follows:

 
User -> SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS) -> Entra ID -> SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS) -> Ariba

CIS authenticates the user before granting access to Ariba.

Test Scenario (Warning)

Step 1 – First Login 

Open Browser Microsoft Edge and log in to SAP Ariba using the test user account.

 

Step 2 – Second Login Attempt

Open Browser Chrome(or another device) and attempt to log in with the same user account.

CIS detects that an active session already exists.

Step 3 – Concurrent Login Detection

The user will see a message as below. This message allows the user to terminate the previous session and continue.

Step 4 – Session Switching

If the user selects Sign out and continue, the system will:

  • Terminate the existing session

  • Allow the new session to proceed

The user will then be logged into SAP Ariba through the new session.

Step 5 – Go back to the old browser and login again, same message showing here.

If the user selects Stay Signed In, the system will not terminate the existing session and show below:

 

Test Scenario (Error)

Configuration change:

 

Step 1 – First Login 

Open Browser Chrome and log in to SAP Ariba using the test user account.

Step 2 – Second Login Attempt

Open Browser Microsoft Edge(or another device) and attempt to log in with the same user account.

CIS detects that an active session already exists.

Step 3 – Concurrent Login Detection

The user will see a message as below. Error shows and no login allowed:

 

Key Benefits of Concurrent Login Control

Using SAP Cloud Identity Services to enforce concurrent login policies provides several benefits.

Improved Security

Restricting concurrent sessions helps prevent credential sharing and unauthorized access.

Centralized Identity Governance

Policies configured in CIS apply consistently across multiple SAP cloud applications.

Better Session Management

Administrators gain better control over authentication sessions and user behavior.

Enhanced Compliance

Session restrictions help organizations meet internal security policies and regulatory requirements.

Useful Documentation

SAP Cloud Identity Services Overview: 

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services

Notes: 

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003327459

Configure Concurrent User Access to the Application:

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/configure-concurrent-user-access-to-application

Conclusion

Concurrent login control is an important security mechanism for managing user sessions and preventing account misuse.

With SAP Cloud Identity Services, administrators can configure and enforce concurrent session policies centrally for SAP cloud applications.

By configuring concurrent login policies and validating them through applications such as SAP Ariba, organizations can strengthen their identity security while maintaining a consistent user authentication experience.

entry.title: Re: Lower case on selection in F4 in RAP Model CDS View
entry.content:
A little late, but I hope it still helps.

You could consider making the selection field searching in lower case.

 

In your CDS metadata this requires a second field used as a search field.

In the CDS projection this requires a second field lower casing the respective text.

Searching in lower case gives results even when they are in upper case.

Of course it would be even better if you could use a custom search help, read the input, and convert it in the background, in ABAP logic.

Now if the user fills in BS (uppercase) the search fails.

I could not find a case online where anyone does that though.

 

At least, you should be able to try this out, and suggest it to the business department, so they can see if they like this suggestion.

entry.title: Joule Analytics Center: Your Go-To Tool for Tracking SAP Joule Usage
entry.content:
SAP Joule is transforming the enterprise experience with its generative AI capabilities, acting as a true AI assistant for your business. As part of the SAP S/4HANA Customer Care and Regional Implementation Group, we often get questions from many customers about how to monitor the usage of Joule when supporting their Joule adoption in S/4HANA.

Indeed, understanding how it's being used is essential for organizations to optimize its usage and align with your AI strategy. This is where the Joule Analytics Center comes in—your central dashboard for measuring its usage and driving user adoption.

This post explains:

  • What Joule Analytics Center is
  • How to enable it
  • How it compares with the Business AI Consumption Dashboard in SAP for Me

What is the Joule Analytics Center?

The Joule Analytics Center is an designed to give administrators a clear, data-backed view of Joule activity within your organization. It provides concrete metrics on user interaction.

With the Joule Analytics Center, you can:

Track Usage: Monitor core metrics like the total number of messages and conversations sent over time. It is possible to define filters on interaction types and product names for which Joule is used, for example, transactional scenarios in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. The definition of each value in the filter bar is available on SAP Help.

The bar charts and the pie charts show the accumulated results based on the date range and filters.

The line charts for Message Counts Over Weeks and Conversation Counts Over Weeks show the trend of Joule usage over time.

Gain Insights: the interactive dashboard helps you to Identify the most common scenarios users are asking about and understand user needs and potential knowledge gaps.

How to Enable the Joule Analytics Center

Activating and accessing the analytics dashboard is a straightforward process for administrators. For the complete, official step-by-step enablement guide, including prerequisites and limitations, please refer to SAP’s dedicated enablement page for Joule Analytics Center.

Joule Analytics Center vs. Business AI Consumption Dashboard

When monitoring the usage of Joule, customers are often interested in the cost occurred as well. It helps to understand the difference in SAP’s AI monitoring tools - Joule Analytics Center and the Business AI Consumption Dashboard in SAP for Me.

Joule Analytics Center

  • Purpose: This is your exclusive tool for operational insights into Joule's direct usage. It answers the question: "How are our employees using Joule?"
  • Metrics: Provides visibility into Joule-specific interactions like message and conversation usage.

Business AI Consumption Dashboard in SAP for Me

  • Purpose: This dashboard offers a financial overview of your spending on advanced AI features. It answers the question, "How many AI Units is our organization consuming across all premium SAP AI services?"
  • Metrics: Tracks the consumption of AI Units against your purchased Premium AI Packages.

A key point to remember is that specific Joule usage data is not displayed within the Business AI Consumption Dashboard in SAP for Me. The Joule Analytics Center is the dedicated and sole source for visibility into Joule's usage in your landscape. While SAP is exploring potential enhancements for broader reporting in SAP for Me in the future, for now, the tools remain distinct.

Conclusion

SAP’s Joule Analytics Center is a cornerstone of effective SAP Business AI governance, as the sole dedicated tool for tracking the foundational message and conversation usage of the Joule generative AI assistant. Paired with the SAP for Me Business AI Consumption Dashboard’s tracking of Premium AI Package AI Units, it creates a holistic monitoring ecosystem.

References 

Joule Analytics Center Overview:

https://help.sap.com/docs/joule/joule-tools-12ace5a963e147ffa6f5aab8a4cf15f4/joule-analytics-center

Enabling the Joule Analytics Center:

https://help.sap.com/docs/joule/joule-tools-12ace5a963e147ffa6f5aab8a4cf15f4/enabling-joule-analytics-center

Help Document for Business AI Tab in SAP for me:

https://support.sap.com/content/s4m/help/finance/business-ai-tab.html

SAP Knowledge Base on Monitoring AI Units Consumption:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3580451

SAP Knowledge Base on Monitoring Joule Consumption:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3604251

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA Customer Care and RIG team.

entry.title: CRA-Certified GST/HST Internet File Transfer Now Available in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.
entry.content:
What's New

CRA-Certified Internet File Transfer (IFT) output for GST/HST

From the GST/HST report in SAP DRC, you can now download a text (.tax) file that meets CRA's Internet File Transfer specifications, then upload it directly through the CRA portal. A new download option appears on the report screen alongside existing exports with no disruption to your current workflows.

Compliance Built In

The generated file automatically follows CRA's required field ordering, delimiter rules (*, ~), and mandatory header information validated during the certification process. Your data is formatted correctly from the start.

Enhanced Report Mappings

Standard content mappings, including tax groups, base vs. tax amounts, and optional fields, have been refined to ensure complete and predictable IFT output, giving you confidence in what gets submitted.

Important: CRA's IFT certification validates only the file layout and CRA system acceptance. It does not certify tax calculations. Tax calculation accuracy remains your responsibility, consistent with CRA's standard testing protocol.

Why This Matters

Faster filing with fewer errors

Upload a system-generated file instead of manually re-entering values into the CRA web form.

Audit trail you can trust

The IFT file is created directly from your authoritative DRC report, giving you complete traceability from source postings through to CRA submission.

Broader eligibility for IFT filing

With the phase-out of the Recapture of Input Tax Credits (RITC) across provinces, more organizations can now file using IFT without requiring Schedule B, expanding the number of organizations that can benefit from this streamlined method.

Availability

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public Edition): Delivered and available since March 14, 2026.
  • SAP S/4HANA On-Premise & Private Cloud Edition: Delivery planned via SAP Note 3708498 in SAP S/4HAA 2025 FPS2; downports planned where applicable

Timelines and scope are subject to change. For final details, follow official SAP What's New releases and SAP Notes.

How It Works

  1. Prepare your return in Manage Document and Reporting Compliance → Canada GST/HST Return (same as today)
  2. Review the preview to confirm boxes, totals, and corrections
  3. Download the new "GST/HST IFT" file (.tax format with deterministic mapping, standard file naming)
  4. Submit through the CRA IFT portal using your 4-digit access code (entered on the CRA site)

Heads up for automation users: If your organization uses UI-sensitive automations (RPA, screen scrapers), be aware that this feature adds a second download line. Review and adjust scripts as needed per standard rollout guidance.

Who Can Use This

  • Eligible: All GST/HST registrants outside Revenu Québec administration
  • Not eligible: Returns requiring Schedules A, B, or C (CRA requires NETFILE for those)

What you’ll see in the product

  • New download line on the GST/HST report for Internet File Transfer (.tax), alongside existing preview/export options.
  • Updated report content to ensure the correct population of mandatory and optional fields before file generation
  • A sample file name (e.g., GSTYYYYMMDD.tax)

Getting Started

Prerequisites

  • SAP DRC for Canada enabled
  • Appropriate role authorizations for Canada statutory reporting
  • Current with relevant SAP Notes and corrections

Documentation

  • "What's New" entries and Help Portal updates will include step-by-step IFT download guidance and known constraints at release
  • CRA's official "Software for GST/HST Internet File Transfer" list links to certified software for use with GST/HST Internet File Transfer.

Regulatory Monitoring & Compliance Governance

  • Track regulatory changes and planned delivery timelines using SAP's Regulatory Change Manager and SAP Road Map Explorer

FAQs

Q: Does CRA certification mean SAP validates my tax calculations?
A: No. CRA's testing confirms that the file format is accepted by CRA systems. Tax calculation accuracy is your responsibility.

Q: Can I still use NETFILE or my bank's EDI service?
A: Absolutely. IFT is an additional option that complements NETFILE, EDI, and other filing methods. Choose whichever approach best fits your profile and requirements.

Q: Do I have to switch to IFT?
A: No. The IFT download is opt-in. Your existing GST/HST report workflows and download options remain unchanged.

Next Steps

  • Join the conversation: Collaborate with us in the Canada Localization Workzone and watch the demo video showcasing the new IFT feature.
  • Explore the enhancement: Review the latest release update to see the feature in action within your system.
  • Stay informed: Check the What’s New entries and SAP Help Portal documentation for detailed guidance and ongoing updates.
  • Get support: Contact your SAP representative if you have questions about eligibility, configuration, or implementation.

Acknowledgments

Thank you to the engineering colleagues and customers who supported content development and validation, as well as CRA testing and certification activities. Your collaboration and feedback directly contributed to the quality and completeness of this delivery.

entry.title: 'Level in Org' field in SuccessFactors via API/Integration Centre
entry.content:
Scenario:

In SuccessFactors Employee Central, Level in Org is a standard field that shows an employee's reporting level (i.e. number of managers or positions above the employee) in an organization. It is a field that is available for reporting purposes and can be accessed in Canvas reports under the entity User → Users Team Info → Level in Org or it can also be calculated in Story Reports using the Create Calculated Column. This field is quite useful for understanding organizational hierarchy and supports various reporting needs. However, despite its usefulness, the standard Level in Org field has several limitations. For one, it is not available via OData API so cannot be queried using OData queries for integrations with third party systems. Additionally, the standard field in Employee Central is evaluated based on Reporting hierarchy rather than Position hierarchy, which will not help in case Employee Central has Position Management implemented, and customer wants to report on Position hierarchy.

The following visual shows how Level in org can be different for the same employee when using Reporting Hierarchy v/s Position Hierarchy.

To address this gap and get the right level in org based on Position Hierarchy, this blog demonstrates how to derive the correct Level in Org based on Position Hierarchy using an integration built in Integration Centre (IC). 

Pre-requisite

Before starting, you must know the total number of levels in your organization. This value will be used in the integration logic.
For this demonstration, we assume the organization has 7 levels and that the topmost position of CEO starts at level 0.

Solution:

  1. Navigate to Integration Centre in Employee Central.
  2. Create a new integration with:
    • Source: SuccessFactors
    • Destination: SFTP
    • Format: CSV
  3. Select JobInfo as the starting entity.
  4. On the field mapping page, map key fields such as Employee ID, First Name, and Last Name from their respective entities.
  5. Click Add Calculated Field.
  6. Add an IF THEN ELSE condition                                                                                                                                                                                     
    • In Parameter 1, click Associate Field and navigate from the employee’s position to parent position 7 times (based on the number of organizational levels).
    • Now check whether the 7th position is not blank.
    • If it is not blank, populate the field value as 7.
    • If it is blank, populate the field value as a hyphen.
  7. Do the IF THEN ELSE logic above a total of 7 times (equal to number of levels in the org) and do the following in each subsequent check:
    • Reduce the number of times you need to navigate from employee's position to parent position by one (e.g., 2nd check should check if the 6th parent position is not blank, 3rd check should check if the 5th parent position is not blank and so on). In essence, if the Nth parent position exists, populate the value as N; otherwise, populate a hyphen into the field value.
  8. At the end of the 7th IF THEN ELSE check, field value contains a string consisting of hyphens and numbers.
  9. After the 7th IF THEN ELSE check, apply the following check as shown in the screenshot below to extract the correct Level in Org value for the employee.

     

  10. Sample value shown in the Level in Org field.

Conclusion:

By using Integration Centre and calculated fields, you can successfully derive an employee’s Level in Org based on Position Hierarchy. This approach overcomes the limitations of the standard field and ensures consistency with organizational structure, even when positions are vacant. Once populated, the Level in Org value can be securely transferred to downstream vendor systems via SFTP or accessed by middleware solutions for further querying via API.

Please let me know what you think of this solution and feel free to share your questions or feedback in the comments section.

Thanks and Regards

Rahul Soneji

entry.title: The SmartRecruiters and SAP SuccessFactors Integration Roadmap
entry.content:
We are excited to share the upcoming roadmap for the productised integration between SmartRecruiters and SAP SuccessFactors HCM, designed to create a unified, end-to-end hiring experience. Our vision is a "Connected Future", where data flows intelligently across systems to power automation and better decision-making. To achieve this, we have outlined a phased evolution that prioritises foundational stability followed by deep automation and advanced analytics.

Phase 1 ✨: Building the Foundations (H1 2026)

The first phase, launched in the first half of 2026, focuses on "The Foundations". This phase is all about creating a seamless user experience and ensuring data alignment. Key highlights include Single Sign-On (IAS) for a unified login experience and a unified UI that allows recruiters to work across systems without switching contexts. Most importantly, Phase 1 establishes the automatic flow of foundational organisational data from SAP SuccessFactors to SmartRecruiters, eliminating manual data entry and ensuring your ecosystem remains consistent.


Phase 2 ✨: Full Integration & Automation (H1 2026)

Phase 2 will introduce "Full Integration and Automation". This stage adds significant depth by automating the conversion of candidates into new hires within SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central and Onboarding. A major benefit for customers is also the ability to run SmartRecruiters and SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting side-by-side, allowing for phased migrations. Additionally, we will introduce an integration offering via SAP SuccessFactors Story Reports, enabling you to build powerful visualisations of your recruitment data directly within SAP SuccessFactors.

Phase 3✨: Elevate and Harmonise (H2 2026 & beyond)

Looking further ahead into H2 2026, our roadmap enters the "Elevate and Harmonise" phase. We plan to deepen our conversational capabilities by connecting Winston Chat and Joule, further streamlining the employee journey. By combining our productised integration with the flexibility of the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), we are ensuring that your talent acquisition suite is not only powerful today but ready for the innovations of your business.

If you would like further information, please reach out to your organisation’s SAP SuccessFactors Partner, Account Executive or Customer Success Manager.

In the meantime, here’s some useful links:

• SmartRecruiters for SuccessFactors
• Integrating SmartRecruiters with SuccessFactors
• Watch an on-demand webinar on SmartRecruiters, an SAP Company

entry.title: Grand Finale VNSG SAP BTP hackathon in a DemoJam
entry.content:
Last year VNSG and SAP BTP product management invited customers and partners to join the hackathon with a real life business challenge. After enablement teams started to work on their case with the support of their coaches and product management. In February the six participating teams presented their solution to the judges and three finalist were chosen.

At the year event of the VNSG and SAP, called SYNC, the finalists presented their solution in a DemoJam. Within 5 minutes they needed to convince the audience during the keynote. After that it was up to the attendees to vote:

🥇  Team Promocean together with SOA People with their Purchase Order Control Center. AI connected to ECC.

🥈Team Ecotone together with Expertum with their Integration Suite Management solution. Joule can now undeploy/deploy groups of iFlows.

🥉Team NTT DATA Business Solutions with Protrack. A solution that enables real time insight for machines on the production floor.

This is a behind the scenes video:

What a great event to be part of. Up to the next one 😀

entry.title: Re: Button “Request SAP Assessment Service“ not visible i...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

based on SAP KBA 3676410 - The "Request SAP Assessment Service" button not available in Rise Quality Gate - SAP Cloud ALM, if the button is not visible at all, check below things which can be a root cause for this:

  • Customer does not have a valid RISE with SAP/SAP Cloud ERP Private contract. This can be checked in the SAP Cloud ALM tenant by going to the RISE with SAP Methodology tab > System View application: if it states “System View dashboard not available”, then the Customer Number associated with the SAP Cloud ALM tenant is not being identified as having a valid RISE with SAP/SAP Cloud ERP Private contract.
  • The tenant is SAP Cloud ALM, tenant extension. In this case use the regular Cloud ALM tenant, not the extension.
  • Automatic Data Replication is "Inactive". This can be checked in the SAP Cloud ALM tenant by going to the Service tab > Service Delivery Center application > Manage Tenant Mapping > Tenant Information. Make sure it is active.
  • Project created without the RISE SAP Activate Roadmaps. Create a Project using a valid Roadmap as per above KBA.
  • Quality Gate was created manually

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: How can I prevent certain users from executing reports in SBO V10 with Coresuite V8.3, while others
Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition 2025
entry.content:
Here’s an overview of the SAP S/4HANA 2025 enhancements for the Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management solution capabilities. More information can be found in the What's New Viewer or in the EPPM roadmap view.

This blog provides summary of the enhancements for each solution area.

Portfolio and Project Management

Project Management

The OData API for Project Management has been enhanced by support of reading distributed values role demand and role staffings.

Also, the archiving of simplified authorizations has been enabled. That is, when a project is archived, the related simplified authorizations are archived.

Portfolio Management

In the area of Portfolio Management, focus of the enhancements as part of SAP S/4HANA 2025 were on the OData APIs and support of planning use cases in combination with SAP Analytics Cloud.

Enhancements to the OData API for ProjectPorfolio:

  • Read portfolio initiative
    Read the master data, status information, object links as well financial and capacity planning information for portfolio initiatives. This includes also the attributes and status information of the initiative’s decision points.
  • Create and update portfolio item
    Create or update portfolio items and their decision points or delete the portfolio item. When using scenarios in which the portfolio item shall be linked to a project in Project System, Project Management or Commercial Project management, the creation of the assigned project can be triggered via specific actions. That is, this is always a 2-step approach, first the item is created, either via API or UI, then the action of the OData API can be used to trigger the project creation. The customizing Map Item Type to Project or PS or Commercial Project is checked to determine the settings for the project creation. If for a item type a default mapping to a Project Template (Project Management)/Project (Project System) exists, this is used for creation. If not, the mapping of item type to project type (Project Management)/project profile (Project System) is used.
  • Support for fiscal-year for financial and capacity planning

Transfer Financial and Capacity Plan Data from SAP Analytics Cloud to Portfolio Management

With SAP S/4HANA 2025 is it possible to enable retrieval of financial and capacity planning values from SAP Analytics Cloud to portfolio items and buckets.

In the example below, the portfolio bucket and item hierarchy are exposed in SAP Analytics Cloud. Financial Views in Portfolio Management (here “02_Approved_Budget) are modelled as versions, categories and groups (for simplification in the example only one category “Budget” with one group “Budget”) are part of the planning model. The user maintains the values in SAP Analytics Cloud. As soon as they are published, they can be considered for transfer.

Example for Financial Planning for Portfolio in SAP Analytics Cloud

 

 

Transfer is leveraging a framework provided by Finance, that has been extended to portfolio financial and capacity planning for buckets and items. In the configuration part of the framework, the SAP Analytics Cloud model is mapped to the portfolio financial and capacity planning structures. Once these configurations are setup, they can be leveraged in scheduled jobs, that are importing the planned data from SAP Analytics Cloud to SAP S/4HANA. These jobs can either be scheduled on the SAP S/4HANA side or they can be triggered by the user or an administrator in SAP Analytics Cloud.

Smaller enhancements have been included based on Continuous Influence Session (3593568) or as continuous improvements. E.g. a new report /RPM/CDOC_ANALYZE, that can be used to do a mass analysis of change documents for portfolio objects (Note 3560661) or fallback mechanism, when creating projects via decision flow management in the context of portfolio item templates (Documentation) .

Released PPM objects as well as which APIs were delivered in which release in available via the Note 3519167.

Project System

OData APIs for Project and Project Network have been extended as part of SAP S/4HANA 2025 by the following enhancements:

  • Object number (OBJNR) has been added for Project and WBS Element
    • For Project Network, the fields ControllingObject and ProjectNetworkObject have been added. ProjectNetworkObject retrieves the OBJNR for the project network. ControllingObject only contains the OBJNR of the project network if the network is header-account assigned.
    • For Network Activity and Network Activity Element, the fields ControllingObject and NetworkActivityObject have been added. NetworkActivityObject retrieves the OBJNR for the network activity. ControllingObject only contains the OBJNR of the network header or activity depending whether the network is header or activity-account assigned.
  • Extend the Project API to support scheduling for a project or its sub-hierarchy, including all assigned activities
  • Create project by copying a standard project template
    In the API a restricted set of fields, such as description, planned start and end date can be provided. The scope of the copying, e.g. shall activities and their depending entities (components, milestones…) be copied, is controlled via flags. To adhere to coding masks, string replacement is provided.
  • Support the inclusion of standard work breakdown structure (WBS) elements in the project hierarchy
    Within an existing project, new WBS elements can be created based on a standard WBS. The new WBS elements can be added under an existing parent or neighbor WBS element in the project. A flag can be leveraged to decide if activities shall be included, string replacement for coding-mask support is also available.

The OData API for ProjectClaim is enhanced to enable reading of partners assigned to the project claim.

Object-based Authorization

Object-based authorizations provide a more granular authorization management based on project object level. It is available for project, WBS, network, network activity and activity element. The details of object-based Authorizations are described in

  • Note 3646104
  • Blog Introducing Object-Based Authorization (OBA) in SAP S/4HANA 2025

If you are currently using Access Control Lists in Project System and want to transition to Object-based Authorizations, see blog Transitioning Projects from ACL to Object-Based Authorization (OBA).  

In addition, some more technical enhancements have been provided for project system.

These include the following:

  • Adjustment to the data retrieval of the Project Schedule (F5611) app based on a new database table for hierarchy read. This new table is automatically filled during upgrade, if manual triggering is required, the report PS_PROJECT_HIERARCHY_REBUILD can be used for selected objects.
  • Additional authorization checks for the Project Schedule (F5611) app
  • Release of Project System objects
    Additional BADIs (e.g. in the area of Dynamic Item Processor) as well as additional CDS views have been release for usage in developer extensibility. The full list of released PS objects is available in Note 3528513. This also includes information which OData API for Project System is available per release.

Commercial Project Management

As of SAP S/4HANA 2025 CPM supports planning of lean services which is described in detail in this separate blog.

It is now also possible to generate Status and Trend Reports in Commercial Project Management in multiple languages (SAP Note 3568205).

entry.title: Debugging Script Tool – Uses and Overview
entry.content:
The SAP ABAP Debugger Scripting Tool is a powerful and advanced feature available in the New ABAP Debugger (introduced from SAP NetWeaver 7.0 onwards). It enables developers to write and execute ABAP code directly within the debugging session. 

Unlike traditional debugging, where developers manually step through the program using F5 (Single Step) or F6 (Execute), the Debugger Scripting Tool allows automation of repetitive or complex debugging tasks. 

It can be considered as a “Debugger for the Debugger”, because it enhances debugging capabilities by enabling: 

  • Automated variable monitoring 
  • Conditional break logic 
  • Mass data validation 
  • Dynamic analysis of internal tables 
  • Runtime data manipulation for analysis 

This significantly improves efficiency when analyzing complex programs, large datasets, or issues that occur inside loops. 

Key Benefits 

  • Automation of Repetitive Checks – No need to manually inspect variables repeatedly inside loops. 
  • Faster Root Cause Analysis – Quickly identify inconsistencies in large internal tables. 
  • Custom Logic Execution During Debugging – Execute custom ABAP logic without modifying the original program. 
  • Time Saving – Reduces manual effort in complex debugging scenarios. 

Inbuilt Scripts (Standard Script Samples) 

SAP provides several Standard Script Samples that are built directly into the debugger. These are pre-delivered template scripts designed to handle common debugging scenarios. 

 Business Scenario: 

How to Stop Execution at a Particular DB Operation (SELECT / INSERT / UPDATE / DELETE) 

In ABAP debugging, sometimes we need to stop execution exactly when a specific database operation is executed — such as a SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE statement. 

This is especially useful when: 

  • Data is not getting updated correctly 
  • Unexpected records are being fetched 
  • Duplicate entries are inserted 
  • Incorrect deletions are happening 

 Example by considering MM03 Transaction Code 
 

Go to Transaction Code MM03. 

Enter the Material Number in the required field. 

Type /h in the command field to activate the debugger. 

 

Click on Enter The system will trigger the debugger.  

Go to the Script tab in the Debugger 

Click on Load Script.  

Click on Overview button  

Expand  Generic breakpoints.  

Select “RSTPDA_SCRIPT_BP_SELECT_TAB”   

Click on Start Script 

Specify database table name where the debugger should stop when a SELECT query runs.  

 

Press the “Continue Script” button every time to continue execution. 
Note: If you press F8, the debugger will stop at each SELECT query. 

By following the same steps, we can try this for other database tables as well.  

 

Similarly, we can use the same approach for other database operations such as INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE. 

 By specifying the required database table and operation type in the script, the debugger will stop whenever that particular INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE statement is executed during program execution.  

Conclusion:

The Debugging Script Tool in SAP is very useful for analyzing database operations during program execution. By specifying the required database table and operation (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE), developers can easily stop the debugger exactly where the database interaction happens. This helps in understanding program flow, identifying issues, and debugging efficiently without manually searching through large amounts of code.

entry.title: Bulk Send Documents to Multiple Signers using Docusign Adapter
entry.content:
The Docusign Adapter for SAP Integration Suite, simplifies and accelerates integration while ensuring secure authentication and connectivity with DocuSign. It enables seamless embedding of e-signature capabilities into your workflows. The adapter streamlines processes such as contract signing, purchase order approvals, and other document-based transactions, resulting in faster processing, enhanced compliance, and a smoother user experience.

Bulk Send using Docusign Adapter

In this scenario, we will demonstrate how to create a template (containing a signable pdf), and send it to multiple signers. We will add the signer's email to the bulk send list used for the bulk send request.

Creating Credentials to connect to Docusign

We will use OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code for this scenario.

OAuth Authentication: The OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow is a secure method for applications, especially server-based ones, to access a user’s data without directly handling their credentials.  For more info, see section 3.3 in the Adapter Guide.

To create the Oauth2 Authorization Code in your SAP environment, specify the artifact Name, Authorization URL, Token Service URL, and the Client ID (Integration Key) from your DocuSign account. Refer figure below to configure the settings.

Iflow Design in SAP CI 

The figure below demonstrates provides a representation of how the adapter works.

Let us break down the three parts to this scenario. 

  • We will create a template in Docusign which has a pdf (encoded as base64); the template must have the signer roles added to it. We will add three roles since we want it to be sent to 3 approvers. You can modify this part to add more.
  • Secondly, we create a bulk send list , which contains the Names and emails of the recepients. Note that the number of signers in the template must match those in the bulk send list.
  • Thirdly, we  use the templateID for step#1 and listId from step#2 to send a bulk request for signature.

Connect to Docusign via Docusign Adapter

Now you must configure the Connection tab of the adapter, specify Address and select Authentication as OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code. Specify the Authorization Code Credentials that was created previously in SAP Security Material. For more information about Connection tab parameters, see Connection in SAP Help.

Convert your PDF to Base64

Create a PDF and convert it into base64; the signer tab position can be set in the payload.

Operation 1:  Create a Template in DocuSign (with a PDF)

In the Content Modifier, you can define the name, description and signer fields.

{
  "name": "Student Activities Form",
  "description": "Form for bulk signing with anchor-based signature fields",
  "emailSubject": "Student Activities Form",
  "emailBlurb": "Dear Student, please enter the details and sign",
  "status": "created",
  "documents": [
    {
      "documentId": "1",
      "name": "Student_Form.pdf",
      "fileExtension": "pdf",
      "documentBase64": "<Your base 64 encoded content goes here>"
    }
  ],
  "recipients": {
    "signers": [
      {
        "roleName": "Signer 1",
        "recipientId": "1",
        "routingOrder": "1",
        "tabs": {
          "signHereTabs": [
            {
              "documentId": "1",
              "tabLabel": "SignatureField",
              "anchorString": "/SignHere/",
              "anchorXOffset": "4.3",
              "anchorYOffset": "10.5",
              "anchorUnits": "cms",
              "anchorIgnoreIfNotPresent": "false"
            }
          ],
          "dateSignedTabs": [
            {
              "documentId": "1",
              "tabLabel": "DateSigned",
              "anchorString": "/DateSigned/",
              "anchorXOffset": "4.3",
              "anchorYOffset": "11.5",
              "anchorUnits": "cms",
              "anchorIgnoreIfNotPresent": "false"
            }
          ],
          "fullNameTabs": [
            {
              "documentId": "1",
              "tabLabel": "FullName",
              "anchorString": "/FullName/",
              "anchorXOffset": "4.3",
              "anchorYOffset": "12.5",
              "anchorUnits": "cms",
              "anchorIgnoreIfNotPresent": "false"
            }
          ],
          "titleTabs": [
            {
              "documentId": "1",
              "tabLabel": "Title",
              "anchorString": "/Title/",
              "anchorXOffset": "4.3",
              "anchorYOffset": "13.5",
              "anchorUnits": "cms",
              "anchorIgnoreIfNotPresent": "false"
            }
          ],
          "textTabs": [
            {
              "documentId": "1",
              "tabLabel": "Name",
              "anchorString": "/Name/",
              "anchorXOffset": "4.3",
              "anchorYOffset": "14.5",
              "anchorUnits": "cms",
              "anchorIgnoreIfNotPresent": "false",
              "required": "false",
              "locked": "false",
              "width": "150",
              "height": "20"
            },
            {
              "documentId": "1",
              "tabLabel": "Value",
              "anchorString": "/Value/",
              "anchorXOffset": "5.3",
              "anchorYOffset": "15.5",
              "anchorUnits": "cms",
              "anchorIgnoreIfNotPresent": "false",
              "required": "false",
              "locked": "false",
              "width": "150",
              "height": "20"
            }
          ]
        }
      }
    ]
  },
  "notification": {
    "useAccountDefaults": "false",
    "reminders": {
      "reminderEnabled": "true",
      "reminderDelay": "2",
      "reminderFrequency": "3"
    },
    "expirations": {
      "expireEnabled": "true",
      "expireAfter": "30",
      "expireWarn": "5"
    }
  }
}

Response: Once the template is created, the response will contain the TemplateId. Store this TemplateId in a header for use in subsequent steps.

Navigate to the next request reply pallet and in processing, select the "Create one or more templates" operation.

Operation 2: Create a Bulk Send List with Recipient Emails

Now, let create a list of receipts to create a bulk send list. In the Content Modifier, you can define and configure the recipient details, such as names, email addresses, or identifiers, to ensure the document is sent to the intended parties accurately and efficiently.

{
  "name": "Student Bulk List - Activities",
  "bulkCopies": [
    {
      "recipients": [
        {
          "roleName": "Signer 1",
          "name": "Signer1",
          "email": "Singer1@example.com"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "recipients": [
        {
          "roleName": "Signer 1",
          "name": "Signer2",
          "email": "Singer2@example.com"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "recipients": [
        {
          "roleName": "Signer 1",
          "name": "Signer3",
          "email": "Singer3@example.com"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

Response: Once the Send List is created, the response will contain the BulkSendListId. Store this ListId in a header for use in subsequent steps.

Navigate to the next request reply pallet and in processing, select the "Create bulk send list" operation.

Operation 3:  Send Template to Multiple Signers/Recipients

Now let us send our document to multiple users.

In a Content Modifier, you need to use the TemplateId that was previously stored in the header.

{
  "envelopeOrTemplateId": "${header.templateID}",
  "mailingListId": "${header.ListId}",
  "senderDisplaySettings": {
    "senderName": "<SenderName>",
    "senderEmailAddress": "<SenderEmail>"
  }
}

Navigate to the next request reply pallet and in processing, select the "Create bulk send request" operation.

Response: Once the Send Request is created, we will receive the requestId to track further.

Result

Once the above process is completed, the signers will receive an email to sign the document.

Click Continue.

Once signed, click Finish. 

Final Thoughts

This Iflow can be modified for scenarios where you want to send documents for a larger list of recipients. For more information, see the Docusign Adapter for SAP Integration Suite.

entry.title: RAP New Feature : Generating Managed RAP Applications With Table Entity
entry.content:
Traditionally, when developing a managed RAP application, we create multiple artifacts such as CDS view entities, behavior definitions, service definitions, and service bindings. However, with the introduction of Table Entity, SAP allows us to directly expose database tables in a RAP-compliant way, significantly reducing boilerplate development effort.

This feature is especially useful when:

  • You want to quickly generate a managed RAP application

  • The table structure already satisfies your business requirements

  • You want to minimize intermediate CDS modeling layers

  • You are building internal or utility applications with simple transactional needs

    In this blog, we will explore:

    • What a Table Entity is in RAP

    • How it differs from traditional CDS view entities

    • Step-by-step creation of a Managed RAP application using Table Entity

    • Service exposure and preview in Fiori Elements

      Table Entity:

      • Read and write access is possible using ABAP SQL SELECT, UPDATE, MODIFY, INSERT, and DELETE.

      • A table entity can be used as a data type in ABAP.

        Properties of Table Entities:

        Key Fields – You can define one or more elements as the primary key using the keyword KEY. However, unlike database tables, key fields are optional and it is also possible to have a table entity without a primary key.

        Note: The access pattern of a table entity without a primary key is that it is always read completely, which can result in longer processing times. Single record access is not provided.

        Client Dependency

        Client handling is defined using the header annotation @ClientHandling.type. The possible annotation values are #CLIENT_DEPENDENT or #CLIENT_INDEPENDENT. 

        Flag for Null Values

        A null value on the database is an undefined value. Columns of table entities are by default created as NOT NULL on the SAP HANA database. It is possible to override this default behavior by using the optional addition NULL for a column. 

        Delivery Class

        The delivery class of a table entity controls the transport of table data in installations, upgrades, or client copies, and in transports between customer systems. Annotation  @AbapCatalog.deliveryClass is used to specify delivery class.

         

        • select table entity template.
@ClientHandling.type: #CLIENT_DEPENDENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass: #APPLICATION_DATA
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'travel information'
define table entity ztb_travel
{
  key travel_id : /dmo/travel_id;
  description   : /dmo/description;
  status        : /dmo/travel_status;
    lastchangedat : timestampl;
 

​

 

  • create report/class to insert entries.

 

select travel_id, description, status,lastchangedat FROM /dmo/travel into TABLE (lt_travel).
if sy-subrc eq 0.
insert ztb_travel FROM TABLE _TRAVEL.
if sy-subrc eq 0.
out->write( 'travel data inserted successfully' ).
else.
out->write( 'failed to insert' ).
ENDIF.
endif.



  • Lets make our table entity as root entity so that will create rap application as well

     
@ClientHandling.type: #CLIENT_DEPENDENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass: #APPLICATION_DATA
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'travel information'
define root table entity ztb_travel
{
  key travel_id : /dmo/travel_id;
  description   : /dmo/description;
  status        : /dmo/travel_status;
    lastchangedat : timestampl;    
}

 

Projection View:

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'consumption view for travel table entity'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
define root view entity zcb_travel as projection on ztb_travel
{

@UI.facet: [{ label: 'Travel Info', type: #IDENTIFICATION_REFERENCE }]
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 10 }]
@UI.identification: [{ position: 10 }]
    key travel_id,
    .lineItem: [{ position: 20 }]
@UI.identification: [{ position: 20 }]
    
    description,
    .lineItem: [{ position: 30 }]
@UI.identification: [{ position: 30 }]
    
    status
}

 

Interface Behavior Definition:

managed implementation in class zbp_tb_travel unique;
strict ( 2 );
with draft;
define behavior for ztb_travel alias tb_travel
lock master total etag lastchangedat
draft table zdtbtrav
authorization master ( instance )
{
  create ( authorization : global );
  update;
  delete;
  field ( readonly ) lastchangedat;
  draft action Activate optimized;
  draft action Discard;
  draft action Edit;
  draft action Resume;
  draft determine action Prepare;
}


Projection Behavior Definition:

projection implementation in class zbp_cb_travel unique;
strict ( 2 );
use draft;

define behavior for zcb_travel alias tb_travel
{
  use create;
  use update;
  use delete;

  use action Activate;
  use action Discard;
  use action Edit;
  use action Resume;
  use action Prepare;
}


Service Definition:

 

@EndUserText.label: 'sd for table entity travel'
define service Zsd_tb_trav {
  expose zcb_travel as TableEntityTravel;
}

 
Service Binding:

 

generate service binding and test,we generate managed rap application, CRUD operations will be handled by framework itself.

Conclusion:
the table entity in RAP provides a simple and effective way to expose database tables directly in RAP-based applications. It helps developers quickly build transactional services with minimal configuration while still benefiting from the RAP framework’s capabilities. By using table entities, developers can accelerate development and maintain consistency within modern SAP application architecture.
ABAP Connectivity  ABAP Extensibility  SAP BTP ABAP environment ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model
